_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: FL House Palm Beach/Broward batch (state tier): Gerwig HD-93 (R), Weinberger HD-94 (R), Hunschofsky HD-95 (D), Daley HD-96 (D), Dunkley HD-97 (D). Evidence from flhouse.gov/flsenate.gov roll calls (HB759 2025 gun-age 21->18), iVoterGuide candidate survey, Florida Right to Life & PBA/FOP endorsements, Equality Florida slates, campaign Issues pages, and members' own floor statements/bill sponsorships. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION captured: Anne Gerwig (R) voted NAY on HB759 (one of only 6 Republicans opposing the gun-age rollback) -> a gun-control-aligned vote scored FALSE on self_defense. Cells with only party-inference or no individual record left null. Dunkley corrected from 'freshman' to a two-term incumbent (since 2022).

profile.background: Florida House District 93 (Palm Beach County), freshman since Nov 2024 (flipped the seat by ~358 votes). Prior office: Mayor of Wellington, FL (2016-2024). Running for re-election 2026 vs. Democrat August Mangeney. NOT seated for any 2023 marquee FL votes.

anne-gerwig@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-93 (R), freshman since Nov 2024; former Mayor of Wellington (2016-2024); running 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Thin first-session record. Life: endorsed by Florida Right to Life (2024 general); no abortion-industry money found. GUN DEVIATION (HARD, highest-value catch): voted NAY on HB759 (2025, House 78-34) lowering the long-gun purchase age 21->18 -- one of only SIX Republicans to oppose the post-Parkland rollback, i.e. she took the gun-control-aligned side. Marriage/sexuality left NULL on directly CONFLICTING municipal-only evidence: as Wellington mayor she cast the lone NAY on a 2017 conversion-therapy ban for minors (conservative-aligned) but in 2015 reversed to vote FOR a municipal LGBT non-discrimination ordinance (liberal-aligned) -- both pre-legislative and contradictory, so neither scored. Sponsored HB261 (2025, repealing the mandated late school-start law) and a Fentanyl Awareness Day bill -- neither maps to a rubric cell. No religious-liberty, election, immigration, federalism, or fiscal-rubric evidence located. Most cells null on an honestly thin record.

0.note: Endorsed by Florida Right to Life (2024 general election) -- HARD evidence of pro-life alignment affirming the rights of the unborn.

4.note: Florida Right to Life-endorsed pro-life candidate; no Planned Parenthood / NARAL / EMILY's List / abortion-industry money found.

1.note: DEVIATION (HARD): voted NAY on HB759 (2025, 78-34) which lowered the long-gun purchase age 21->18 -- one of only 6 Republicans to oppose, i.e. she voted to KEEP the post-Parkland age restriction (gun-control-aligned).

profile.background: Florida House District 94 (Palm Beach Gardens / NW Palm Beach County), freshman since Nov 2024. Running for re-election 2026. Rich self-stated positions via campaign site and her own iVoterGuide candidate survey.

meg-weinberger@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-94 (R), freshman since Nov 2024; running 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative; positions sourced from her OWN campaign Issues page and iVoterGuide survey answers (HARD self-statements), plus PBA/FOP endorsements and two confirmed roll calls. Life: 'support the sanctity of human life and the rights of the unborn'; opposes taxpayer funding for Planned Parenthood. Marriage/sexuality: defines marriage as 'a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman,' opposes transgender participation in women's sports/spaces and taxpayer-funded gender transition; NOT on Equality Florida's 2025 slate. Education: backs school choice ('dollars to follow the child') and parental authority. Christian liberty: opposes forcing businesses to provide services that violate religious beliefs and opposes government church closures. Elections: opposes voting without photo ID, wants ineligible/non-citizen voters purged. Public justice: endorsed by Palm Beach County PBA and FOP. Border: 'strongly agree' on securing the southern border and bans sanctuary cities. Guns: voted YEA on HB759 (2025) lowering the gun-purchase age and 'strongly opposes restrictions on lawful gun ownership.' No cross-party deviation found. Fiscal/federalism slogans ('combat unconstitutional overreach,' tax cuts, Gulf-of-America rename) left null -- they do not map to the specific state economic/federalism rubric cells.

0.note: Campaign Issues: 'Support the sanctity of human life and the rights of the unborn' -- her own pro-life self-statement.

4.note: iVoterGuide: opposes taxpayer funding for abortion providers / Planned Parenthood; no abortion-industry money found.

0.note: Defines marriage as 'a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman' -- her own statement.

2.note: Opposes transgender participation in women's sports/spaces and taxpayer-funded gender transition -- affirms biological sex.

4.note: Opposes taxpayer-funded gender transition and trans participation in women's sports -- opposes LGBTQ-ideology promotion in public policy.

0.note: Supports school choice -- 'dollars to follow the child' -- and parents guiding their children's education by their values.

1.note: Opposes forcing businesses to provide services that violate their religious beliefs -- supports conscience exemptions for Christian business owners.
Opposes voting without photo ID and wants non-citizen/ineligible voters purged from the rolls -- supports photo ID with citizenship verification.

0.note: Endorsed by the Palm Beach County PBA and the Fraternal Order of Police; campaign 'support our men and women in uniform' -- publicly backs law enforcement.
'Strongly Agree' the U.S. should physically secure the southern border; supports securing the border.

2.note: Supports banning sanctuary cities -- opposes sanctuary policies.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB759 (2025) lowering the long-gun purchase age 21->18; 'strongly opposes restrictions on lawful gun ownership.'

profile.background: Florida House District 95 (Broward -- Coconut Creek, Margate, Parkland, parts of Coral Springs), since 2020. Former Mayor of Parkland (2016-2020), in office during the 2018 MSD shooting. Currently Minority Leader pro tempore and House Democratic Leader-designate. Running for re-election 2026 vs. Republican Allison Boettcher.

christine-hunschofsky@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-95 (D), since 2020; House Democratic Leader-designate; running 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive; flagship gun-control profile from her Parkland-mayor background. Life: opposed the 2023 6-week abortion ban (SB300), calling it a 'de facto abortion ban' and 'literally the antithesis' of a free state (HARD floor statement). Education: vocal public-school advocate, named Broward PTA's 2026 'Legislator of the Year,' sponsored a school-counselor bill (HARD). Guns (all HARD): publicly opposed 2023 permitless carry HB543 ('This is not constitutional carry. This is untrained carry.'); led opposition to lowering the gun-purchase age 21->18 (HB759); prime sponsor of HB321 (2026) barring long guns from 'sensitive' government spaces; as Parkland mayor lobbied for stricter background checks; aligned with Moms Demand Action. Marriage/sexuality, religious liberty, elections, immigration, federalism, public-justice cells left NULL -- only SOFT party-line inferences (no individual roll call or personal statement isolated). No conservative cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Opposed the 2023 6-week ban (SB300), calling it a 'de facto abortion ban' and 'the antithesis' of a free state -- documented pro-choice, does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Opposite of abortion abolition: publicly fought the 6-week ban.

0.note: Public-school champion (Broward PTA 2026 Legislator of the Year, sponsored a school-counselor bill); aligned with the Democratic caucus opposing universal vouchers -- opposite of supporting universal school choice.
Publicly opposed 2023 permitless carry (HB543): 'This is not constitutional carry. This is untrained carry.'

1.note: Prime sponsor of HB321 (2026) barring long guns from 'sensitive' spaces and led opposition to lowering the gun-purchase age -- pro-gun-restriction record.

profile.background: Florida House District 96 (Broward -- Coral Springs/Sunrise), since a 2019 special election; prosecutor; former Coral Springs City Commissioner. Passed on a 2026 SD-30 Senate bid to run again for HD-96 (his final term before term limits).

dan-daley@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-96 (D), since 2019; prosecutor; running again 2026 (final term). [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Strongest documented record is gun-control. Guns (all HARD): sponsor of 'Jaime's Law' (background checks on ammunition purchases, named for a Parkland victim) and, in the 2026 session, an assault-weapon / 10+-round-magazine ban plus ammo background checks; publicly fought 2023 permitless carry HB543, calling it 'a dangerous political stunt to appease the NRA far right'; opposed lowering the gun-purchase age 21->18 (HB759), saying 'every single time we do this... it reopens wounds.' Life (SB300), marriage (HB1521), education (HB1), and immigration (SB1718) left NULL -- only near-party-line tallies, no individually named roll call or personal statement isolated for him. As a prosecutor and proponent of bolstering the FL National Guard, his only conservative-adjacent notes are institutional (SOFT), not ideological. No conservative cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Publicly fought 2023 permitless carry (HB543), calling it 'a dangerous political stunt to appease the NRA far right' -- opposite of supporting constitutional carry.

1.note: Sponsored 'Jaime's Law' (ammo background checks) and a 2026 assault-weapon + 10+-round-magazine ban -- pro-restriction sponsorship record.

profile.background: Florida House District 97 (Broward -- Lauderdale Lakes, Lauderhill, North Lauderdale, Sunrise). TWO-TERM incumbent: first elected 2022 (succeeding Dan Daley after redistricting), re-elected 2024; running for a third term 2026. Retired U.S. Army (Iraq tour); born Kingston, Jamaica.

lisa-dunkley@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-97 (D), two-term incumbent since 2022 (NOT a freshman -- record corrected); running 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Guns (HARD): voted NAY on HB759 (2025, House roll call) -- opposed lowering the long-gun purchase age 21->18, the standard Democratic gun-control position. Sponsored career-education (HB553, Purple Star military-family campuses), housing/affordable-housing (HB627, expanded Hometown Heroes), and tax-exemption (HB185) bills -- none of which map to the specific state economic/family rubric cells. Not on Equality Florida's 2025/2026 slate; no Planned Parenthood endorsement found; co-sponsored a uterine-fibroid research-database bill (women's health, not abortion). Life, marriage, education-choice, election, immigration, public-justice, and federalism cells left NULL -- no individually documented position located. No conservative cross-party deviation found. Genuinely thin scoreable record; scored the one HARD vote and nulled the rest.

1.note: Voted NAY on HB759 (2025, 78-34) -- opposed lowering the long-gun purchase age 21->18, i.e. voted to keep the post-Parkland age restriction (gun-control-aligned).
